beg.desc: Every C program starts with the main() function. #include brings in libraries.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Understanding C program structure and entry point.

int.desc: Use command-line arguments and return codes for flexible program behavior.
int.usecase: Real-world use: CLI tools with argument parsing.

pro.desc: Use _Noreturn, __attribute__, and proper exit handling for production C programs.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Signal handling for graceful daemon shutdown.

beg.desc: C has basic types: int, float, double, char. Variables must be declared with a type.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Storing different kinds of data in programs.

int.desc: Use sizeof, limits.h, and type qualifiers for precise type control.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Portable code with fixed-width integer types.

pro.desc: Use _Generic for type-generic macros and _Alignas for memory alignment control.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Cache-aligned data structures for high-performance computing.

beg.desc: C supports arithmetic, relational, logical, and bitwise operators.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Arithmetic calculations and logical checks.

int.desc: Use bitwise operators for flags, masks, and efficient bit manipulation.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Unix file permission flags.

pro.desc: Use compound assignment, comma operator, and ternary for concise expressions.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Efficient bit manipulation in embedded systems.

beg.desc: printf outputs formatted text. scanf reads user input.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Interactive command-line programs.

int.desc: Use format specifiers for precise output formatting and safe input handling.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Formatted table output and safe input reading.

pro.desc: Use snprintf for safe string formatting and custom output formatting functions.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Safe JSON-like serialization for logging.

beg.desc: Use if/else and switch to control program flow based on conditions.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Grading systems and menu selections.

int.desc: Use switch with enums for type-safe state machines.
int.usecase: Real-world use: State machines in embedded firmware.

pro.desc: Use function pointer dispatch tables for efficient multi-branch logic.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Calculator engines and opcode dispatch tables.

beg.desc: Loops repeat code blocks. C has for, while, and do-while loops.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Iterating through data and countdowns.

int.desc: Use nested loops for matrix operations and break/continue for flow control.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Matrix operations and prime number computation.

pro.desc: Use Duff's device and loop unrolling for optimized iteration in performance-critical code.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Optimized memory operations in system programming.

beg.desc: Functions break code into reusable pieces. Prototypes declare them before use.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Organized, modular C programs.

int.desc: Use const parameters, static functions, and pass-by-pointer for output parameters.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Functions with multiple return values via pointers.

pro.desc: Use variadic functions and function attributes for flexible, optimized APIs.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Type-safe variadic logging functions.

beg.desc: Arrays store fixed-size sequences of elements of the same type.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Storing collections of numeric data.

int.desc: Use 2D arrays and pass arrays to functions with explicit size parameters.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Matrix operations in numerical computing.

pro.desc: Use VLAs and flexible array members for runtime-sized data structures.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Variable-length protocol buffers in network programming.

beg.desc: C strings are null-terminated character arrays. Use string.h functions to work with them.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Text processing in C programs.

int.desc: Use strncpy, strtok, and strstr for safe string operations and parsing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: CSV parsing and text search.

pro.desc: Use snprintf-based string builders for safe, efficient string construction.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic string building for log messages and JSON output.

beg.desc: Pointers store memory addresses. Use & to get address and * to dereference.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Understanding memory access in C.

int.desc: Use pointers for array traversal and function argument passing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Swap operations and array processing via pointers.

pro.desc: Use void pointers and restrict keyword for generic programming and optimization.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Generic algorithms like qsort in the C standard library.

beg.desc: Pointer arithmetic lets you navigate through memory using addition and subtraction.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Navigating arrays with pointer offsets.

int.desc: Use pointer arithmetic for string processing and buffer manipulation.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Implementing string functions from scratch.

pro.desc: Use pointer subtraction for measuring offsets and implement memory views.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Zero-copy buffer slicing in network protocol parsing.

beg.desc: Use malloc to allocate memory at runtime and free to release it.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Creating arrays of runtime-determined size.

int.desc: Use calloc for zeroed memory and realloc for resizable arrays.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic arrays (vectors) implemented in C.

pro.desc: Use arena allocators for batch allocation with O(1) cleanup.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Arena allocators in game engines and compilers.

beg.desc: Structs group related variables together into a single type.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Representing entities like students and employees.

int.desc: Use struct pointers, designated initializers, and nested structs.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Geometric data structures with nested types.

pro.desc: Use opaque structs and handle patterns for information hiding and API design.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Opaque types for library API design (like FILE* in stdio).

beg.desc: Enums define named constants. Unions share memory between members.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Named constants for state and options.

int.desc: Use unions for type-punning and variant types with tagged unions.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic value types in interpreters and config parsers.

pro.desc: Use anonymous unions and compound literals for ergonomic variant types.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: AST nodes in compilers and expression evaluators.

beg.desc: Use fopen, fprintf, fscanf, and fclose for file reading and writing.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Reading and writing configuration files.

int.desc: Use binary file I/O for structured data persistence.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Binary data serialization for save files.

pro.desc: Use memory-mapped files for zero-copy file processing.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Processing multi-GB log files with zero-copy I/O.

beg.desc: The preprocessor handles #include, #define, and conditional compilation.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Constants and utility macros.

int.desc: Use conditional compilation and include guards for platform-portable code.
int.code:
#ifndef CONFIG_H
#define CONFIG_H

#ifdef _WIN32
  #define PATH_SEP '\\'
  #define EXPORT __declspec(dllexport)
#else
  #define PATH_SEP '/'
  #define EXPORT __attribute__((visibility("default")))
#endif

#ifdef DEBUG
  #define LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, "[DBG] " fmt "\n", ##__VA_ARGS__)
#else
  #define LOG(fmt, ...) ((void)0)
#endif

#endif // CONFIG_H
int.usecase: Real-world use: Cross-platform header files with debug logging.

pro.desc: Use X-macros for generating repetitive code from a single source of truth.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Auto-generated error codes and string tables.

beg.desc: Header files (.h) declare functions. Source files (.c) implement them.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Multi-file C project organization.

int.desc: Use extern, static, and inline for controlling symbol visibility across translation units.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Controlling visibility in multi-file projects.

pro.desc: Use weak symbols and constructor/destructor attributes for plugin systems.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Shared library initialization and plugin architectures.

beg.desc: Bitwise operators work on individual bits: AND, OR, XOR, NOT, shifts.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Working with hardware registers and protocols.

int.desc: Use bit manipulation for efficient set operations and flag management.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Permission flags in operating systems.

pro.desc: Use intrinsics and bit tricks for high-performance algorithms.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Fast math in game engines and signal processing.

beg.desc: Recursion is when a function calls itself. Always define a base case.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Mathematical computations.

int.desc: Use recursion for tree traversal and divide-and-conquer algorithms.
int.usecase: Real-world use: O(n log n) sorting with merge sort.

pro.desc: Convert recursion to iteration with explicit stacks for deep call chains.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Stack-safe quicksort for large datasets.

beg.desc: Function pointers store the address of a function, enabling callbacks.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Selecting operations at runtime.

int.desc: Use function pointers for callbacks and plugin-style architectures.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Event handler registration systems.

pro.desc: Use vtables (arrays of function pointers) to implement polymorphism in C.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: OOP vtable polymorphism in C (used in GTK, Linux kernel).

beg.desc: A linked list is a chain of nodes, where each node points to the next.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic data storage without fixed size limits.

int.desc: Implement insert, delete, and reverse operations for linked lists.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Classic interview problem: reverse a linked list.

pro.desc: Use intrusive linked lists for zero-allocation container patterns.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Linux kernel's list_head intrusive linked list.

beg.desc: Stacks follow LIFO (Last In, First Out). Queues follow FIFO (First In, First Out).
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Undo operations and expression evaluation.

int.desc: Implement a circular buffer queue for efficient FIFO operations.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Task scheduling and message queues.

pro.desc: Use lock-free ring buffers for concurrent producer-consumer scenarios.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Lock-free queues in audio processing and trading systems.

beg.desc: C requires manual memory management. Always free what you malloc.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Proper memory lifecycle management.

int.desc: Use valgrind-friendly patterns and RAII-like cleanup with goto.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Centralized cleanup with goto in system programming.

pro.desc: Implement a simple memory pool for predictable allocation performance.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Fixed-size memory pools in game engines and embedded systems.

beg.desc: C uses return values and errno for error reporting.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Diagnosing file operation failures.

int.desc: Use custom error codes and error propagation patterns.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Structured error handling in library APIs.

pro.desc: Use setjmp/longjmp for exception-like error handling in C.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Non-local jumps for error recovery in parsers.

beg.desc: System calls interact directly with the operating system kernel.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Time measurement and benchmarking.

int.desc: Use POSIX file operations and directory traversal for system utilities.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Directory listing tools like ls.

pro.desc: Use fork/exec for process management and pipes for inter-process communication.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Process spawning and IPC in Unix daemons.
